codeReview.summary: This fix directly addresses both blocking findings from m7-clock-skew-and-leak: long-session success criteria are tightened to require per-iteration sign+ECDH completion (with 64-hex ECDH shared secret validation), and the asymmetric ±120s case is now explicitly represented as a skipped, rationale-documented protocol deviation linked to runtime-deviations-from-paper.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The recurring protocol constraint that drives this fix (bifrost-signer `max_future_skew_secs=30`, and why asymmetric ±120s must be treated as deviation) is documented in app docs but not in `.factory/library`, so future workers may repeatedly rediscover it during planning/review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Constraint is documented in `docs/runtime-deviations-from-paper.md` and referenced in `src/e2e/multi-device/clock-skew.spec.ts`, while grep over `.factory/library` for `max_future_skew_secs|VAL-CROSS-019|clock skew|LONG_SESSION_ITERATIONS` returns no matches.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the fix transcript skeleton and both diffs (`e93c09b` original + `0fffdc9` fix), and the fix adequately resolves the prior blocking failures: it enforces all-iteration sign/ECDH success with per-iteration shared-secret shape checks, and it documents/implements the asymmetric ±120s case as an explicit, protocol-constrained skipped deviation tied to VAL-CROSS-019/027.
